title: Rashi of Troyes: The Man Whose Commentary You Can't Study Without
title_zh: 特鲁瓦的拉什:没有他的注释就无法学习的伟人
title_he: רש"י מטרואה: האיש שבלי פירושו אי אפשר ללמוד
author: Torah Light Editorial Team
target_audience: Chinese native Mandarin speakers with no prior Jewish knowledge
chinese_cultural_parallels: Zhu Xi (朱熹) and the tradition of annotated classics
Comparing Torah commentary to Chinese classical annotation tradition
The Analects (论语) with scholarly commentaries

historical_period.jewish: Medieval French Jewry, Golden Age of Ashkenaz

key_figures: Zhu Xi (朱熹, for cultural parallel)
bibliography_sources: Traditional Rashi commentaries on Chumash and Talmud
Historical sources on Medieval French Jewry
notes: Article draws strong parallel between Rashi and Zhu Xi's role as annotators of classical texts. Emphasizes accessibility and pedagogical clarity. Includes detailed explanation of Rashi's methodology and lasting influence. Chinese historical context (Northern Song Dynasty) integrated throughout.
